mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly reducing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window or positive returns exceed negative returns in greater than 65 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 55 percent of triggered instances over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth combined with stable consumer price inflation indicates robust consumer demand without monetary policy tightening pressure, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Interaction term loses statistical significance with p-value greater than 0.10 in multivariate regression or signal accuracy drops below 50 percent in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and corporate earnings momentum, generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns during economic recovery phases.
failure_condition: Signal produces negative forward returns in greater than 60 percent of recovery phase observations or fails to outperform buy-and-hold over 10-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly increases corporate profit expectations and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over any 3-year rolling window or forward returns show no statistical difference from zero with t-test p-value greater than 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in greater than 58 percent of triggered instances or signal accuracy falls below 45 percent in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and housing starts indicates synchronized economic strength across labor and construction sectors, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Joint signal loses predictive power when either series is removed or produces negative returns in greater than 62 percent of co-occurring positive growth months.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle signals supply-side contraction and earnings deterioration, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Signal generates positive forward returns in greater than 55 percent of tightening cycle observations or fails to achieve statistical significance in regime-filtered backtests.
